and list any inconsistency or defect in the documentation so far 
before writing any code


Orchestrated technical specification review and documentation audit
Orchestrated technical specification review and documentation audit

Checking for any skill relevant to authoring markdown specification documents before creating files

Script

Creating the technical specification for the restaurant management system, derived from REQUIREMENTS.md, including the normalized schema requested in §10
TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION.md

Check which output files already exist and transcript size

Script

Review first part of transcript for requirements details

Script

Review remainder of transcript for spec structure and tool calls

Script

Creating the documentation review cataloguing all inconsistencies and defects found in REQUIREMENTS.md and dump.txt, cross-referenced from the technical specification
DOCUMENTATION_REVIEW.md

Presented 2 files
Done
Both documents are ready. I'd suggest committing them as docs/TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION.md and docs/DOCUMENTATION_REVIEW.md — tracked, and deliberately outside docs/llm/ so they survive your dump exclusions.

The specification is implementation-ready: single Blazor Server app with four areas, ASP.NET Identity over custom Dapper stores with .NET 10's built-in passkeys, a fully relational append-only order event log (class-table inheritance, no EAV, no JSON) with the complete DDL and projection views, DbUp migrations, in-container pg_dump backups on a systemd user timer, OTel via standard OTEL_* variables, and a thin CI that only calls scripts/continuous_integration.sh. Every place your requirements conflicted or were silent, the spec records the interpretation it chose and cross-references a finding ID so you can veto it before any code exists.

The review catalogues 33 findings. Five are genuine contradictions where I need a ruling:

F‑01: Can counter actually reset passwords (§3.3), or is §4.5's admin-only rule authoritative? Spec assumes admin-only.
F‑02: Price adjustment — counter only (§6.3) or kitchen too (§6.5)? Spec assumes counter + admin.
F‑03: First-admin auto-grant can't satisfy the passkey+TOTP-at-grant mandate; spec resolves it with a /setup wizard that enforces both before granting. OK?
F‑04: "Only counter can close" (§5.4) vs admin full authority (§4.3) — spec allows either.
F‑05: Counter has no passkey requirement while kitchen does, despite handling money. Intentional?
The largest technical gap is F‑06: the requirements never address TLS, yet passkeys, Wake Lock, and audio all demand HTTPS — and passkeys bind to the domain, so quick tunnels' random hostnames silently kill any passkey registered through one. The spec closes this with a Caddy internal CA on a stable LAN hostname, a named tunnel for production, and quick tunnels demoted to demo-only with a printed warning.

On dump.txt itself: export.sh includes itself twice (roughly doubling the dump), REQUIREMENTS.md isn't in the dump at all — likely untracked or hiding in the excluded docs/llm/ — and the tee/mv combination has a flush race that can leave the output file momentarily truncated despite the atomic-rename intent. Details and fixes are in Group D.

Give me your five rulings and I'll fold them into both documents and draft the seven initial ADRs.


Technical specification
Document · MD 

Documentation review
Document · MD 

Now using credits • Your plan limit resets Jul 16 at 8:30 AM.






Claude is AI and can make mistakes. Please double-check responses.
